14 Inital survival Analysis

Katie M Susong

03-June-22

Overview

Inital plots and correlation for survival/hatch rate.

Plots and Analysis

  1. histogram of percent survival - the distribution is skewed to 0, given bounding at 0 and 1 pdf = beta

  2. GDDs

    1. Plots
      1. histogram
      2. correlation scatter plots
    2. Correlation tests
    3. Regression
      1. null model: per.sur.live ~ 1
      2. GDDdjf10
      3. GDDfull10

  3. Mean Jan T

rm (list  = ls())

#libraries# 
library(ggplot2)
library(betareg)
library(sjPlot)
library(patchwork)

#### Functions ####
overdisp_fun <- function(model) {
  rdf <- df.residual(model)
  rp <- residuals(model,type="pearson")
  Pearson.chisq <- sum(rp^2)
  prat <- Pearson.chisq/rdf
  pval <- pchisq(Pearson.chisq, df=rdf, lower.tail=FALSE)
  c(chisq=Pearson.chisq,ratio=prat,rdf=rdf,p=pval)
}


#### Import data ####
setwd("~/Documents/CBS_PhD/Ae.albo_OW_2021/21.22_Ae.albopictus_Overwintering")
# import data avg. to the tire level 
tire <- read.csv("00_Data/21.22_bytire.hatch_temperature_summary_NO.Arl3.csv")
# format corrections
tire <- subset(tire, select = -c(X))
tire$site    <- factor(tire$site, levels = c("", "Car", "Bon", "Dek", "Arl", "Han", "Spo"))
tire$loc.id <- factor(tire$loc.id, levels = c("CTR",
                                              "IL_Car_T1", "IL_Car_T2",
                                              "IL_Bon_T1", "IL_Bon_T2",
                                              "IL_Dek_T1", "IL_Dek_T2",
                                              "WI_Arl_T1", "WI_Arl_T2",
                                              "WI_Han_T1", "WI_Han_T2",
                                              "WI_Spo_T1" ,"WI_Spo_T2"))

# import data avg. to the site
site <- read.csv("00_Data/21.22_bysite.hatch_temperature_summary_NO.Arl3.csv")
# format
site$site    <- factor(site$site, levels = c("CTR", "Car", "Bon", "Dek", "Arl", "Han", "Spo"))
site <- subset(site, select = -c(X))


##### 1. histogram of percent survival #####

1. histogram of percent survival

Positive skewed pdf. Large number of 0s. could be rare event zero inflation. (Ref. for zero inflation here )

hist(tire$per.sur.live)

2. histogram of different GDDs

minimal distribution. high or low value

par(mfrow=c(2,2))
hist(tire$GDD_10_DJF)
hist(tire$GDD_n12_DJF)
hist(tire$GDD_10_FULL)
hist(tire$GDD_n12_DJF)

par(mfrow=c(1,1))

3. GDDs v survival

a. Plots

p1 <- ggplot(tire, aes(GDD_10_DJF, per.sur.live, col = site)) +
  geom_point()+
  ggtitle("GDD_10_DJF")

p2 <- ggplot(tire, aes(GDD_n12_DJF, per.sur.live, col = site)) +
  geom_point() +
  ggtitle("GDD_n12_DJF")

p3 <- ggplot(tire, aes(GDD_10_FULL, per.sur.live, col = site)) +
  geom_point() +
  ggtitle("GDD_10_FULL")

p4 <- ggplot(tire, aes(GDD_n12_FULL, per.sur.live, col = site)) +
  geom_point() +
  ggtitle("GDD_n12_FULL")

p1 +p3 + p2+ p4
## Warning: Removed 1 rows containing missing values (geom_point).

## Warning: Removed 1 rows containing missing values (geom_point).

## Warning: Removed 1 rows containing missing values (geom_point).

## Warning: Removed 1 rows containing missing values (geom_point).

FIGURE NOTES:

  - Car (the site with the highest survival) has a much larger number of GDDs 
  regardless of metric
  
  - Over all there does not seem to be a strong trend for the corr between sur. and 
  any GDDs

b.Correlation tests

# Pearson correlation test for each GDDs metric
cor.test(tire$per.sur.live, tire$GDD_10_DJF, method = "pearson")
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  tire$per.sur.live and tire$GDD_10_DJF
## t = 5.6089, df = 10, p-value = 0.0002249
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.5942836 0.9633794
## sample estimates:
##       cor 
## 0.8710907
cor.test(tire$per.sur.live, tire$GDD_n12_DJF, method = "pearson")
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  tire$per.sur.live and tire$GDD_n12_DJF
## t = 5.6089, df = 10, p-value = 0.0002249
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.5942836 0.9633794
## sample estimates:
##       cor 
## 0.8710907
cor.test(tire$per.sur.live, tire$GDD_10_FULL, method = "pearson")
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  tire$per.sur.live and tire$GDD_10_FULL
## t = 3.7045, df = 10, p-value = 0.004078
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.3312713 0.9289808
## sample estimates:
##       cor 
## 0.7605746
cor.test(tire$per.sur.live, tire$GDD_n12_FULL, method = "pearson")
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  tire$per.sur.live and tire$GDD_n12_FULL
## t = 3.7045, df = 10, p-value = 0.004078
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  0.3312713 0.9289808
## sample estimates:
##       cor 
## 0.7605746

Correlations Test Notes

 - For all measurements of GDD: Per.sur is significantly correlated to the 
 number of GDDS

c. Models

# return to 2,2 plots of model test plots 
par(mfrow=c(2,2))

Null model

# null model 
null <- glm(per.sur.live ~ 1, data = tire)
summary(null)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = per.sur.live ~ 1, data = tire)
## 
## Deviance Residuals: 
##      Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max  
## -0.12221  -0.12221  -0.12221   0.06241   0.54313  
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
## (Intercept)  0.12221    0.05967   2.048   0.0631 .
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for gaussian family taken to be 0.04629345)
## 
##     Null deviance: 0.55552  on 12  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 0.55552  on 12  degrees of freedom
## AIC: -0.093965
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 2

GDD (set to temperatures => 10 ??C) during DJF

GDDdjf10 <- glm(per.sur.live ~ GDD_10_DJF, data = tire) # GDD (djf 10??C)
summary(GDDdjf10)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = per.sur.live ~ GDD_10_DJF, data = tire)
## 
## Deviance Residuals: 
##      Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max  
## -0.10772  -0.08577  -0.03167   0.02564   0.19905  
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 0.0115457  0.0393490   0.293 0.775202    
## GDD_10_DJF  0.0051676  0.0009213   5.609 0.000225 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for gaussian family taken to be 0.01300895)
## 
##     Null deviance: 0.53934  on 11  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 0.13009  on 10  degrees of freedom
##   (1 observation deleted due to missingness)
## AIC: -14.239
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 2
# measures of fitness 
plot(GDDdjf10)          # fitness plots

overdisp_fun(GDDdjf10)  # overdisp. function 
##       chisq       ratio         rdf           p 
##  0.13008945  0.01300895 10.00000000  0.99999999

Model fitness

  - Residuals vs Fitted: even variance, homoscedasticity
  - Normal Q-Q:  1.0 + quarterly showing some non-linearity
  - Scale-Location: some pattern in the residuals, generally horizontal
  - Residual vs Leverage: Obs. 4 and 5 are influential
  - over dispersal: Not significantly overdisp.

GDD (set to temperatures => 10 ??C) during full time

GDDfull10 <- glm(per.sur.live ~ GDD_10_FULL, data = tire) # GDD (full 10C)
summary(GDDfull10)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = per.sur.live ~ GDD_10_FULL, data = tire)
## 
## Deviance Residuals: 
##      Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max  
## -0.16889  -0.08257  -0.03359   0.03649   0.28459  
## 
## Coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)   
## (Intercept) -0.1128371  0.0792269  -1.424  0.18483   
## GDD_10_FULL  0.0011270  0.0003042   3.704  0.00408 **
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for gaussian family taken to be 0.02273462)
## 
##     Null deviance: 0.53934  on 11  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 0.22735  on 10  degrees of freedom
##   (1 observation deleted due to missingness)
## AIC: -7.5397
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 2
plot(GDDfull10)

overdisp_fun(GDDfull10)
##       chisq       ratio         rdf           p 
##  0.22734619  0.02273462 10.00000000  0.99999986

Model fitness

  - Residuals vs Fitted: 
  - Normal Q-Q: 
  - Scale-Location: 
  - Residual vs Leverage: 
  - over dispersal: 
tab_model(GDDfull10)
  per.sur.live
Predictors Estimates CI p
(Intercept) -0.11 -0.27 – 0.04 0.185
GDD_10_FULL 0.00 0.00 – 0.00 0.004
Observations 12
R2 Nagelkerke 0.584

GDD (set to temperatures => -12 C) during DJF time

#### 4. Survival v Mean Jan tire temperature ####

ggplot(tire, aes(JANmeanT, per.sur.live, col = site))+
  geom_point()
## Warning: Removed 1 rows containing missing values (geom_point).

#### 5. Survival v DaysB12T

ggplot(tire, aes(DaysB12T, per.sur.live, col = site))+
  geom_point()
## Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values (geom_point).

#### 5. Survival v hrB12Tcon

ggplot(tire, aes(MAXHrsB12conT, per.sur.live, col = site))+
  geom_point()
